purpose: Focused large-model comparison lane for Gemma 4 MoE/QAT CPU-MoE sidecar runs against nearby dense or standard single-host counterparts.

rounds.description: One filtered build task per runnable candidate. Validates endpoint, template, assertions, and artifact writing.
All 10 direct suite tasks. Use this before Promptfoo full assertions because direct suite is deterministic and faster.
Long-context needle, repo evidence audit, and harness code-review pressure tasks for candidates that clear round2.
Incremental config tweaks only when a concrete failure suggests the tweak: context, reasoning, KV cache, chat template, max_tokens, and CPU-MoE/gpu-layer flags.

pairs.question: Does the 26B A4B QAT CPU-MoE sidecar materially beat the dense/standard 12B class on the full local workflow suite and long-context retrieval?

counterparts.reason: Nearest already-installed dense/standard 256k Gemma 4 class in the registry.
HF QAT GGUF counterpart for apples-to-apples Unsloth QAT packaging; no CPU-MoE needed unless metadata proves expert tensors.

pairs.question: Can the 31B QAT family be made practical with llama.cpp sidecar tuning, and does it beat the standard 31B LM Studio lane?

moeCandidate.reason: QAT packaging may improve practicality versus the existing 31B-QAT LM Studio timeout/failure evidence.

counterparts.reason: Already-installed standard 31B lane; one narrow build row previously passed.
Already-installed QAT lane; prior evidence is timeout/failure-heavy and needs sidecar comparison.

pairs.question: Do smaller Gemma 4 efficient variants benefit from CPU-MoE style serving, or are their existing GPU-fit LM Studio profiles already enough?

moeCandidate.reason: Already-installed efficient Gemma 4 control with 10/40 historical Promptfoo assertions.

counterparts.reason: Already-installed efficient Gemma 4 control with 11/40 historical Promptfoo assertions.

decisionRules: Do not promote from a single filtered build task.
A candidate must beat or match 8/10 direct full suite, pass a long-context needle task, and produce reviewable outputs before shortlist promotion.
Only tune a config when the previous artifact identifies a concrete failure class.
Keep one model server loaded at a time; stop llama-server before LM Studio load tests and unload LM Studio before llama-server tests.
Archive each round artifact under results/large-moe-* before changing config.
